summary.brief: Hackett v. Bybordi. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: According to the plaintiffs, on November 1, 2010, the decedent was treated in the Hospital's emergency room by Ilbris Yarde.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On November 20, 2010, the decedent died from 'pulmonary thromboembolism due to pelvic and lower extremity deep vein thrombosis complicating obesity (one month status post induced vaginal delivery of 27 week stillborn).'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Yarde discharged the decedent from the Hospital with an upper respiratory infection diagnosis, and sent her home.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The requisite elements of proof in a medical malpractice action are a deviation or departure from accepted standards of medical practice, and evidence that such deviation was a proximate cause of injury.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Yarde's expert did not address the plaintiffs' allegations that Yarde failed to adhere to the Hospital's own guidelines for the prevention of venous thromboembolism.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, the Supreme Court properly denied the stated portions of Yarde's motion for summary judgment dismissing the third-party complaint.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient presented with symptoms consistent with an upper respiratory infection.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

critical_findings: Risk factors for DVT and PE

reveal.decision_impact: Incorrect discharge without addressing DVT/PE risk

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with symptoms and risk factors for DVT/PE
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Initiate workup for DVT/PE including imaging and anticoagulation
options.clinical_reasoning: Patient had risk factors for DVT/PE which required further investigation

options.description: Discharge with diagnosis of upper respiratory infection
options.clinical_reasoning: Failed to address risk factors for DVT/PE

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant discharged patient with upper respiratory infection diagnosis

expected_action_court.description: Workup for DVT/PE should have been initiated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Hospital guidelines for prevention of venous thromboembolism

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failed to adhere to hospital guidelines for DVT/PE prevention

patient_outcome.description: Patient died from pulmonary thromboembolism due to DVT

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to diagnose and treat DVT/PE
Causation established - failure to diagnose led to death

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to diagnose and treat DVT/PE
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Timely diagnosis and treatment could have prevented death
